readinessCheck: tenant-context readiness kind (always ready once resolved)
v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved
acceptablePartialRouteReason: Tenant record + FQDN lifecycle is fully delivered and proven (proof:domain-identity-matrix; proof:tenant-custom-domain-resolution); provisioning operates via the system-admin surface. A richer self-service provisioning UI is not part of this capability — the '(partial UI)' note is cosmetic, not a closure gap.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Tenant host identity lifecycle is explicit: apex/reserved/invalid/malformed hosts resolve no tenant, seeded slug FQDNs resolve to hostSource=slug, and active verified custom domains resolve to hostSource=custom_domain. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/domain-identity-matrix-runtime-proof.ts and tenant-custom-domain-resolution runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model includes organisation id, slug/FQDN, realmName tenant-{organisationId}, hostSource slug|custom_domain|fixture, and tenant_domains registry states for custom FQDNs. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/tenant-resolver.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Tenant provisioning is operator-controlled by platform.tenants.read/create; host identity read is deterministic request context and does not grant data access without route permission checks. Evidence: capability record and apps/platform-api/src/server/pipeline.ts tenant resolution flow.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is POST /api/admin/tenants for provisioning and GET /api/host-identity for request host identity; runtime resolution consumes Host/X-Forwarded-Host. Evidence: capability record, routes, and domain-identity-matrix proof.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Host validation classifies apex, slug, local slug, reserved, unknown, malformed, host-with-port, and custom-domain candidates; X-Forwarded-Host precedence is proven. Evidence: packages/domain-identity/tests/domain-identity.test.ts and domain-identity-matrix runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Invalid, unknown, reserved, apex, inactive custom, pending custom, and disabled custom hosts resolve to null tenant context rather than a partial or fallback tenant. Evidence: tenant-resolver tests and tenant-custom-domain-resolution runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Provisioning is audited at the tenant provisioning surface; host identity resolution itself is read-only and has no mutation audit. Evidence: capability auditAction and tenant resolver/pipeline read-only path.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready once the resolver and registry are available; proofs skip honestly only when required seeded/local substrate is absent and do not fake tenant resolution. Evidence: domain-identity-matrix and tenant-custom-domain-resolution runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs: proof:domain-identity-matrix validates classification plus live slug resolution; proof:tenant-custom-domain-resolution validates live tenant_domains resolution, inactive/pending/disabled rejection, and unique enabled-domain guard. Proof level: 4 live substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI generation must treat tenant identity as a context primitive: display current tenant/host source, distinguish apex/global vs tenant contexts, expose provisioning only to platform tenant operators, and never infer a tenant from invalid or untrusted hosts.

v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Membership lifecycle is explicit: invite validates and creates pending invitation for a new user or direct membership for an existing user, invited resolves to active on login, role/status/username can be updated, disabled can be re-enabled, and remove deletes the membership. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/members.ts and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is tenant-scoped Membership with userId, organisationId, tenant role, optional username, status invited|active|disabled, createdAt, updatedAt, lastLoginAt, invitedBy, plus pending invitation rows. Evidence: packages/domain-identity/src/index.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes require tenant.members.read, tenant.members.invite, tenant.members.update_role, or tenant.members.delete; manager has legacy member.* bundle only and does not receive tenant.members.* route permissions. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is GET /api/org/members, POST /api/org/members/invite, PATCH/DELETE /api/org/members/:userId, PATCH username/status, POST resend-invite, and GET external-identities. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Email format is validated and lowercased; system-admin is rejected as a tenant membership role; username format and status transitions are explicit; last tenant-admin cannot be demoted, disabled, or removed. Evidence: members unit tests and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes include invalid_body, conflict, already_invited, not_found, last_admin_cannot_be_demoted, last_admin_cannot_be_removed, last_admin_cannot_be_disabled, invalid_transition, and ok; routes map them to 400/409/404/422/204/201 responses. Evidence: members usecase and routes.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Member invite, role change, username/status changes, resend invite, and remove emit audit only after validation and precondition reads; audit failure aborts mutation and conflict paths emit no misleading audit. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Membership readiness is invariant-ready once tenant context and Postgres identity tables are available; list reads use tenant context and pending invitation queries always filter by organisation_id. Evidence: members usecase comments and tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts, members-v2.test.ts, packages/domain-identity/tests/domain-identity.test.ts; substrate proof: postgres-identity-repository and organisation profile/member route coverage in test:platform-api. Proof level: 3 state-machine/permission validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose member and pending-invitation tables, invite form, role/status/username edit actions, resend and remove actions, external identity read-only details, last-admin guard states, duplicate/conflict states, validation errors, permission-denied states, and audit-before-change expectations.

v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Profile lifecycle is default -> updated own profile; preferences lifecycle is default empty -> updated enabled/disabled channel/category preferences -> read back; notification dispatch uses enabled preferences as sent and disabled preferences as suppressed. Evidence: profile, notifications unit tests, profile-self-service and notification-preferences runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Profile state is tenant+user scoped displayName, locale, timezone; preference state is tenant+user scoped channel, category, enabled; dispatch state is durable log status sent|suppressed|failed. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/ports/profile-repository.ts and notification-repository.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Self-service reads use profile.read_self and writes use profile.update_self; userId is the authenticated session subject and never a path/body parameter. Operator notification test uses platform.notifications.read/write outside this self-service surface. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts and notification-dispatch runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contracts are GET/PATCH /api/me/profile and GET/PATCH /api/me/notification-preferences; notification readiness/test contracts support the preference proof. Evidence: routes.ts and docs/api/openapi.json.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Profile update rejects empty displayName; route schema rejects invalid profile/preferences payloads; dispatch rejects secret-bearing payload fields; PATCH without tenant context returns NO_TENANT. Evidence: profile unit tests and notification-dispatch runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Validation failures return safe VALIDATION_ERROR/ValidationError responses; no-tenant profile/preference writes return 400 NO_TENANT; secret-bearing notification payloads raise api.error.secretFieldNotNotifiable. Evidence: profile.ts, notifications.ts, and route proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Profile updates emit profile.updated on user_profile before change; preference updates emit notification preferences audit; operator test sends emit notification.tested. Evidence: profile and notifications unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Profile and preferences are always-on once the Postgres substrate is migrated; live proofs skip honestly if Postgres is unavailable and verify no secret-bearing columns plus RLS tenant isolation. Evidence: profile-self-service and notification-preferences runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/profile.test.ts and notifications.test.ts. Runtime proofs: proof:profile-self-service, proof:notification-preferences, proof:notification-dispatch. Proof level: 4 live Postgres/RLS and live route-handler validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose Account profile fields displayName/locale/timezone, notification preference channel/category toggles, read defaults, update validation, own-user-only semantics, no-tenant/operator default states, permission-denied states, and no secret-bearing payload display.

v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: API key lifecycle is explicit: create returns a plaintext secret once, list exposes metadata only, authenticate validates live non-revoked secret, revoke sets revokedAt and authentication then returns null. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/api-keys.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State is active vs revoked, with createdAt, lastUsedAt, optional expiresAt, revokedAt, keyPrefix, scopes, and hash/salt storage fields. Plaintext secret is not a persisted state. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/ports/api-key-repository.ts and api-keys unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Tenant operations use tenant.api_keys.read/write and operator read uses platform.api_keys.read; creation also requires api_access entitlement deny-by-default. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/api-key-routes-runtime-proof.ts and api-keys unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contracts are GET/POST /api/org/api-keys, DELETE /api/org/api-keys/:keyId, GET /api/org/developer, and GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/api-keys. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/api-key-routes-runtime-proof.ts and docs/api/openapi.json.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Creation rejects tenants without api_access; invalid key IDs reject in route proof; garbage/wrong secrets never authenticate; tenant B cannot list tenant A keys. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/api-keys.test.ts and api-key-routes runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Usecase result variants include not_entitled and ok; invalid route IDs return 400; revoked, wrong, hash-presented, or malformed secrets authenticate as null. Evidence: api-keys unit tests and api-key-routes runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Mutations emit api_key.created and api_key.revoked audit events before change; failing audit aborts creation before storage. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/api-keys.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Runtime readiness is proof:api-keys plus proof:api-key-routes. The live Postgres proof skips honestly when the substrate is unavailable and never fake-passes. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/api-keys-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/api-keys.test.ts. Runtime proofs: apps/platform-api/scripts/api-keys-runtime-proof.ts and apps/platform-api/scripts/api-key-routes-runtime-proof.ts. Proof levels: 4 live substrate for repository/crypto/RLS and 4 live route-handler validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose key list metadata, create form with name/scopes and secret reveal-once, revoke action, developer entitlement/active-count summary, operator read-only tenant key view, permission-denied states, and no secret/hash in list surfaces.

route: admin UI semantics proven via the Semantic Reference Harness (proof:ui-semantic-groups)
v2Target: reuse-and-extend: V1 API+permissions+contract are the semantic source; V2 must add the missing admin UI slice (no behaviour change)
acceptablePartialRouteReason: UI semantic gap closed by the Semantic Reference Harness headless journey (proof:ui-semantic-groups) — machine-readable definition + passing journey — establishing the V2 build contract (states/permissions/validation/a11y). A shipped V1 admin page is intentionally out of scope for this semantic closure.
closedBy: V1-completion acceleration slice — Semantic Reference Harness

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Group lifecycle is explicit: list existing Keycloak groups, create after name validation and duplicate check, update after existence and conflict checks, and delete after protected-name guard. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/groups.ts and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/groups.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Group state is Keycloak group id, name, and path in the tenant realm; reserved names are protected and groups are not persisted in Postgres. Evidence: GroupsAdapterPort and groups unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes require tenant.groups.read/create/update/delete, are tenant-scoped, and declare organisation:groups UMA resource and matching scopes. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts and groups unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contract surface is GET/POST /api/org/groups and PATCH/DELETE /api/org/groups/:groupId with tenant credential-backed KeycloakRealmAdminAdapter. Evidence: routes.ts and groups usecase.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Group names must be strings, non-empty, <=64 chars, no path separators/control characters, not reserved, and not duplicate case-insensitively. Evidence: groups unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes include invalid_name, conflict, not_found, protected, and ok; routes map these to 400, 409, 404, 422, 201, and 204. Missing tenant credential returns NO_CREDENTIAL/503. Evidence: groups usecase and routes.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Create/update/delete emit GroupCreated, GroupUpdated, and GroupDeleted after validation/precondition checks and before Keycloak mutation; audit failure aborts adapter mutation. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/groups.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready for semantics, with route execution requiring tenant context plus auth-settings credential for the tenant realm; the UI semantic harness proves the V2 build contract without shipping a V1 page. Evidence: route credential checks and proof:ui-semantic-groups.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/groups.test.ts. UI semantic proof: proof:ui-semantic-groups. Proof level: 3 behaviour/state validation plus semantic harness for future UI generation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose group list, create/update/delete actions, reserved/protected and duplicate-name states, tenant credential unavailable state, permission gates, validation messaging, and accessible table/form/action semantics from the Semantic Reference Harness.

route: admin UI semantics proven via the Semantic Reference Harness (proof:ui-semantic-sub-organisations)
v2Target: reuse-and-extend: V1 API+permissions+contract are the semantic source; V2 must add the missing admin UI slice (no behaviour change)
acceptablePartialRouteReason: UI semantic gap closed by the Semantic Reference Harness headless journey (proof:ui-semantic-sub-organisations) as a FLAT tenant-scoped list — parent_id is internal-only and not exposed by the API, so no hierarchy UI was invented. A shipped V1 admin page is intentionally out of scope for this semantic closure.
closedBy: V1-completion acceleration slice — Semantic Reference Harness

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Sub-organisation lifecycle is explicit: list active child organisations, create with slug/displayName, update displayName, and deactivate by setting is_active false. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/sub-organisations.ts and sub-organisations unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State is public.organisations row with parent_id, slug, display_name, is_active, created_at, and updated_at; the API exposes a flat tenant-scoped list and keeps parent_id internal. Evidence: sub-organisations usecase and capability acceptablePartialRouteReason.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes require tenant.suborgs.read/create/update/delete, are tenant-scoped, and declare organisation:sub-organisations UMA resource/scopes. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contract surface is GET/POST /api/org/sub-organisations and PATCH/DELETE /api/org/sub-organisations/:subOrgId. Evidence: routes.ts and sub-organisations usecase.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Create validates lowercase slug length/characters/no edge hyphen, displayName length, reserved slugs, and duplicate global slug; update requires at least displayName and validates length. Evidence: sub-organisations usecase and unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes include invalid_body, reserved_slug, conflict, not_found, and ok; routes map these to 400, 422, 409, 404, 201, 200, and 204. Evidence: routes.ts and sub-organisations tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Create, update, and deactivate emit SubOrganisationCreated/Updated/Deactivated before DB mutation; audit failure aborts insert/update/deactivation. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/sub-organisations.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready once Postgres organisations table is available; organisations has no RLS, so every query explicitly filters by parent_id from the FQDN-resolved tenant context. Evidence: sub-organisations usecase comments and tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/sub-organisations.test.ts. UI semantic proof: proof:ui-semantic-sub-organisations. Proof level: 3 state-machine/validation proof plus semantic harness for future UI generation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose a flat sub-organisation table, create and displayName update forms, deactivate action, reserved/duplicate/not-found states, parent-internal semantics, permission gates, validation messaging, and accessible table/form/action states from the Semantic Reference Harness.

v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: RBAC lifecycle is role assignment plus deterministic permission resolution: tenant membership roles are tenant-admin, manager, member, viewer; system-admin is a global role and is rejected as a membership role; role changes use the member role update path. Evidence: packages/domain-identity/src/index.ts and members tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is AnyRole split into GlobalRole system-admin and TenantRole tenant-admin|manager|member|viewer, with role bundles resolved by ROLE_PERMISSION_MAP and membership status tracked separately. Evidence: packages/domain-identity/src/index.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Permissions are the enforcement primitive; roles are convenience bundles. Routes enforce explicit requiredPermission values such as tenant.members.*, tenant.groups.*, tenant.suborgs.*, platform.* and never the role name directly. Evidence: domain-identity comments and routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract is the role/permission vocabulary exported by @platform/domain-identity and drift-checked against contracts-admin TENANT_ROLES/MEMBERSHIP_STATUSES; member role routes are PATCH /api/org/members/:userId and related role/status surfaces. Evidence: contracts-admin-drift and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.validation: validateMembership requires userId, organisationId, role, and rejects system-admin; resolvePermissions tests prove role bundles and split platform.admin.access vs tenant.admin.access. Evidence: packages/domain-identity/tests/domain-identity.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Invalid role updates return invalid_body, missing memberships return not_found, last-admin demotion/removal/disable return explicit 422 validation outcomes, and permission denial is handled by the pipeline before route handlers. Evidence: members usecase, routes, and api-pipeline tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: RBAC mutations are audited through member role/status update audit events; audit failure aborts membership role changes before DB mutation. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready because permission resolution is pure and covered by domain tests; route enforcement is exercised through API pipeline permission tests. Evidence: domain-identity tests and apps/platform-api/tests/substrate/api-pipeline.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: packages/domain-identity/tests/domain-identity.test.ts, apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts, contracts-admin-drift tests. Proof level: 3 semantic role/permission state validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must derive role pickers from TENANT_ROLES, never offer system-admin as a tenant role, display permission-denied states from requiredPermission, enforce last-admin guard messaging, and keep role labels separate from route permission strings.

v2Target: refine-and-complete: V1 proven core is the base; close the production-blocker gap in V2 before cut
acceptablePartialRouteReason: The runtime policy-evaluation seam is delivered and proven; the remaining generalised PDP vocabulary is a V2 extension, not a V1 cut blocker.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Policy lifecycle is read current resource policy -> audit -> set replacement policy; request evaluation is UMA-first when a token exists, static fallback on unavailable/unregistered resource, policy-denied fail-closed, and step-up on insufficient auth level. Evidence: authorize-resource and resource-policies unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is route guard resource + umaScope + requiredPermission, optional ResourcePolicy records, UMA decision granted|policy_denied|insufficient_auth_level|keycloak_unavailable|resource_not_registered, and static permission fallback. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/authorize-resource.ts and tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes declare explicit requiredPermission, resource, and umaScope; resource policy writes are behind tenant auth-settings permission and use audit-before-adapter mutation. Evidence: routes.ts, authorize-resource tests, and resource-policies tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is /api/auth/settings/resource-policies plus per-route resource/umaScope guard metadata consumed by the pipeline and GraphQL authorizer. Evidence: routes.ts and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/resource-policies.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Unknown or unregistered UMA resources degrade only to the static permission backstop; absent static permission denies; injected resource policy may allow without static permission; wildcard resource policy names are accepted. Evidence: authorize-resource and resource-policies tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Policy denied maps to 403, insufficient_auth_level maps to stepUpRequired/401, missing token when one is expected maps to authenticationRequired/401, and static fallback without permission maps to 403. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/authorize-resource.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Resource policy mutation emits audit before adapter set; audit failure prevents adapter mutation. Authorization checks are read-only and logged/returned rather than audited per request. Evidence: resource-policies unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready for the local policy seam; Keycloak unavailable and resource_not_registered are explicit degraded/non-decisive states with static fallback rather than fake allow. Evidence: authorize-resource tests and API pipeline UMA tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/authorize-resource.test.ts and resource-policies.test.ts. Runtime proof: proof:entitlement-policy-chain covers permission-before-entitlement policy ordering. Proof level: 3 state/policy-chain validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose resource-policy read/write surfaces only to permitted auth-settings actors, show resource/scope/permission vocabulary, present step-up/forbidden/unavailable states distinctly, and never imply UMA grants when static fallback is the actual decision source.

evidence: ADR-0063; ADR-ACT-0304; migration 037; proof:delegated-admin; delegations.test.ts; postgres-delegated-admin-roles.test.ts
v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Delegation lifecycle is grant -> active, duplicate active grant -> delegation_already_active with no mutation, revoke -> revoked, list tenant delegations for authorised operators, and self-only active delegation lookup for non-admin grantees. Evidence: delegations unit tests and proof:delegated-admin.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is DelegatedRole with id, organisationId, granterUserId, granteeUserId, scope, grantedAt, grantedBy, expiresAt, revokedAt, and revokedBy; active means not revoked and not expired. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/ports/delegated-admin-roles.ts and runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Operator routes require platform.delegations.read/write; grant is allowed for system-admin or tenant-admin contexts, revoke is system-admin only in current scope, non-admin self-lookup is limited to their own grantee id. Evidence: delegations unit tests and routes.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contract surface is POST and GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/delegations plus DELETE /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/delegations/:delegationId. Evidence: capability record and routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Non-admin grant/list/revoke fails closed; duplicate active grant short-circuits; server overwrites client-supplied grantedBy with ctx.userId; tenant isolation keeps ORG_A grants from ORG_B. Evidence: delegations unit tests and runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes include ok, static_permission_denied, delegation_already_active, and not_found; denied paths log warnings and emit no audit or mutation. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/delegations.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Grant emits Delegation.Granted before port.grant and audit failure prevents persistence; revoke emits Delegation.Revoked after successful revoke; listForTenant emits Delegation.Listed on authorised path; no-op denied/not_found paths emit no audit. Evidence: delegations unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready for the usecase contract; local runtime proof exercises the composition root against in-memory port/audit and Postgres adapter tests cover storage behaviour. Evidence: proof:delegated-admin and postgres-delegated-admin-roles tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/delegations.test.ts and postgres-delegated-admin-roles.test.ts. Runtime proof: proof:delegated-admin. Proof level: 3 local state-machine and audit-order validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose operator tenant delegation list, grant form with grantee/scope/expiry, revoke action, duplicate-active/no-op states, denied states, self-only grantee read semantics, server-stamped grantedBy display, and audit ordering expectations.

readinessCheck: n/a (always-on once built; entitlement reads are deterministic)
v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Entitlement lifecycle is explicit: absent/not_granted denies by default, operator grant moves a key to granted, operator revoke moves it to revoked, and revoked/absent both block access. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/entitlements.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is organisationId + entitlementKey with state granted|revoked plus source, metadata, updatedAt, and updatedBy; tenant self-read projects catalog keys as granted, revoked, or not_granted. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/ports/entitlement-repository.ts and entitlements unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Tenant read requires tenant.entitlements.read; operator read/write require platform.entitlements.read/write; policy-chain evaluation denies before entitlement when the caller lacks permission. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/entitlements-routes-runtime-proof.ts and apps/platform-api/scripts/entitlement-policy-chain-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contracts are GET /api/org/entitlements, GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/entitlements, PATCH /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/entitlements, and service-catalog read. Evidence: entitlements-routes runtime proof and docs/api/openapi.json.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Unknown entitlement keys are rejected without audit; invalid tenant ids return 400; feature flags are not entitlements; cross-tenant grants do not leak. Evidence: entitlements unit tests and entitlements-routes runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: assertEntitlement throws typed ForbiddenError when not entitled; evaluateEntitlement returns denied decisions by permission or entitlement; route invalid tenant/key cases return 400. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/entitlements.test.ts and entitlements-routes runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Grant and revoke emit entitlement.granted / entitlement.revoked audit events before mutation; failing audit blocks persistence and leaves rows unchanged. Evidence: entitlements unit tests and entitlements-postgres runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Entitlement reads are deterministic once the Postgres substrate is migrated; live readiness is proven by proof:entitlements-postgres, which verifies table existence, forced RLS, no secret columns, tenant isolation, operator bypass, grant/revoke, and audit-before-change.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/entitlements.test.ts. Runtime proofs: proof:entitlements, proof:entitlement-policy-chain, proof:entitlements-postgres, proof:entitlements-routes. Proof levels: 3 for local state/policy proofs and 4 for live Postgres/routes.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose tenant read-only entitlement catalog states, operator grant/revoke actions with note/metadata, unknown-key rejection, permission-denied states, audit-before-change expectation, and no secret-bearing fields in entitlement/service-catalog surfaces.

v2Target: reuse-and-extend: V1 support-session issuance plus approval workflow are the semantic source; V2 can regenerate the visual layer only
acceptablePartialRouteReason: Support-session issuance plus the approval workflow are delivered and proven locally; host-origin escalation remains a separate policy gate.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Support access lifecycle is request approval -> workflow waiting, approve -> workflow completed -> audited one-hour support session created for exactly one target tenant; direct enterSupportMode creates the same scoped session after validation. Evidence: support-mode unit tests and support-approval runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is support workflow id/status, support session id, supportMode true, effectiveOrganisationId, targetOrganisationId/tenantId, supportAccessReason, roles, ttlSeconds=3600, and no tenant-scoped permissions. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/support.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Route requires platform.admin.access and platform:support enter semantics; usecase enforces system-admin role defence-in-depth and canAccessTenantFqdn only allows support-mode system-admin on the matching effective tenant. Evidence: support-mode tests and pipeline predicate.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is POST /api/admin/support-session and the support approval workflow proof using requestSupportApproval and approveSupportApproval. Evidence: support usecase and proof:support-approval.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Non-system-admin, empty reason, whitespace reason, and empty targetOrganisationId are rejected before audit/session creation; support-mode actor without effectiveOrganisationId cannot access tenant FQDN. Evidence: support-mode unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Errors include support_mode.forbidden, support_mode.reason_required, support_mode.invalid_target, wrong-tenant FQDN denial, and audit backend failure preventing session creation. Evidence: support-mode tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Support session creation emits support_session.created before session creation; approval request emits audit with requested state and approval workflow id; audit failure creates no support session. Evidence: support.ts and support-mode tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is hermetic-domain ready for approval and support-session semantics; workflow waits for approval and completes on approval signal, with no live external provider claim. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/support-approval-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/support-mode.test.ts. Runtime proof: proof:support-approval. Proof level: 3 local workflow/session state-machine validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose support access request/approval state, required reason, target tenant, one-hour window, matching-tenant-only access, audit status, denied/non-admin states, and avoid granting tenant permissions through support mode.

v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Privileged audit lifecycle is read-only contextual query over durable audit rows: choose logical resource, map to stored resource and per-context permission, query tenant-scoped audit events, redact secret metadata, and return bounded results. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/audit.ts and audit unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is AuditEventDto with id, action, actorId, resource, resourceId, timestamp, and redacted metadata; logical resources map to member/config/feature/auth_settings contexts. Evidence: audit usecase.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Route requires coarse tenant.audit.read and the usecase enforces per-context permissions such as tenant.members.read, tenant.config.read, tenant.features.read, and tenant.auth.settings.read. Evidence: routes.ts and audit unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contract is GET /api/org/audit with resource, resourceId, action, actorId, from, to, and limit query parameters; tenant id comes from the resolved session tenant, never from the frontend. Evidence: routes.ts and audit usecase.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Unknown logical resources are invalid; missing per-context permission is forbidden; limit is clamped to 1..200; resourceId/action/actor/date filters are optional. Evidence: audit usecase and tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes are ok, forbidden, and invalid; route maps invalid to 400, forbidden to 403, and ok to 200. Evidence: routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: The capability reads existing audit events and is not itself a mutation audit; secret-ish metadata keys are redacted and ip/userAgent are not exposed. Evidence: audit usecase comments and tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready for contextual audit reads; the broader live history proof validates read-only projection, source isolation, secret exclusion, pagination, and no mutation against live Postgres. Evidence: proof:history.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/audit.test.ts. Runtime adjacent proof: proof:history validates live read-only audit/history projection. Proof level: 4 for live history projection plus 3 for contextual audit unit semantics.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose audit panels with logical resource filters, resource/action/actor/time filters, bounded pagination, forbidden/invalid states, tenant-only results, redacted metadata, and no mutation controls on read-only audit events.

capability: Custom domains, DNS ownership, TLS, canonical
v2Target: reuse-as-is
acceptablePartialRouteReason: DNS ownership, auth-client activation, local routing proof, and canonical cutover are all proven locally; public DNS/TLS remain external infra limits, not V1 cut blockers.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Custom domain lifecycle is explicit: create challenge -> pending_dns, verify DNS TXT -> verified, activate auth client -> active, prove local routing -> routing_local_active, set/unset canonical, deactivate clears auth/canonical/routing, disable frees the enabled claim while retaining history. Evidence: tenant-domains, tenant-domain-canonical, tenant-domain-claim-lifecycle, and tenant-domains-routing runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Registry state includes ownershipStatus pending_dns|dns_mismatch|verified, authClientStatus inactive|active, routingStatus routing_unknown|routing_local_active, tlsStatus tls_unknown, canonical flag, redirectPolicy no_redirect|redirect_slug_to_canonical, timestamps, disabledAt, and challenge token rows. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/ports/tenant-domain-registry.ts and migrations.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Domain operations are tenant-scoped by tenant.domains.read/write; auth-client mutation is performed through the injected AuthClientDomainPort after ownership is verified. Evidence: capability record and apps/platform-api/src/usecases/tenant-domain-lifecycle.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contract surface is /api/org/domains*, including challenge creation, verification, activation/deactivation, routing probe, canonical set/unset, and list/readiness projection. Evidence: capability record, docs/api/openapi.json, and domain route/runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Domain validation rejects invalid names and IP literals; DNS verification requires the expected _aldous-verify TXT token; canonical is refused until verified, active, and locally routed; cross-tenant enabled-domain claims are rejected before token issuance. Evidence: vanity-domain-challenge and tenant-domain-canonical/claim-lifecycle proofs.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes include invalid_domain, domain_already_claimed, not_found, expired, already_verified, dns_not_found, dns_mismatch, not_verified, auth_client_inactive, routing_not_proven, not_active, and ok. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/vanity-domain-challenge.ts and tenant-domain-lifecycle.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Mutations emit safe metadata-only events for challenge creation, conflict rejection, verification, auth-client activation/deactivation, local routing proof, and canonical set/unset; conflict paths never leak DNS tokens. Evidence: tenant-domain-claim-lifecycle proof and tenant-domain-lifecycle unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness distinguishes no_domains, pending_dns, verified, inactive auth-client, local routing proven, public DNS/TLS deferred, and canonical redirect cutover; local proofs explicitly do not claim public DNS/TLS readiness. Evidence: tenant-domains and tenant-domains-routing runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs: proof:tenant-domains, proof:tenant-domains-routing, proof:tenant-domain-canonical, proof:tenant-domain-claim-lifecycle, proof:tenant-custom-domain-resolution, and proof:tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin. Proof level: 4 live substrate/route-handler validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose domain list/readiness, challenge creation with TXT record, verify/retry states, activation/deactivation, local routing status, canonical set/unset, conflict and invalid-domain errors, deferred public TLS/DNS labels, permission-denied states, and no token leakage after creation.

capability: Data governance: catalog, lineage, classification, PII, DSR/GDPR
v2Target: reuse-and-extend: catalogue, lineage, PII classification, and DSR workflow are now semantic V2 source

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: DSR lifecycle is explicit and proven: createDsr creates state=open; fulfillDsr transitions open -> fulfilled exactly once; repeated fulfillment rejects. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/data-governance.test.ts; apps/platform-api/scripts/data-governance-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Dataset classifications use none|pii|sensitive and DSR records use open|fulfilled; migration 041 defines CHECK constraints and migration 045 adds fulfillment_evidence. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/db/migrations/041-data-governance.sql; apps/platform-api/src/db/migrations/045-data-governance-fulfillment-evidence.sql.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Admin governance routes require platform.governance.* and DSR route semantics are recorded on this capability; route registration is proven in apps/platform-api/scripts/data-governance-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is /api/admin/governance/catalog, /api/admin/governance/catalog/classify, /api/admin/governance/dsr, and /api/admin/governance/dsr/:dsrId/fulfill. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/data-governance-runtime-proof.ts and docs/api/openapi.json.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Owner normalization, lineage de-duplication/sorting, deterministic PII/sensitive sample classification, and already-fulfilled rejection are proven. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/data-governance.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Invalid second fulfillment is rejected with not found or already fulfilled; deterministic classification falls back to none.no_match when no rule matches. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/data-governance.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Capability audit action is DSR fulfilment audited; fulfilled records persist fulfilled_by, fulfilled_at, and fulfillment_evidence. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/ports/data-governance.ts; apps/platform-api/src/adapters/postgres-data-governance.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Runtime readiness is the proof:data-governance command, which verifies route registration, classification semantics, catalogue lineage, and DSR fulfillment evidence. Evidence: package.json script proof:data-governance.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/data-governance.test.ts. Runtime proof: apps/platform-api/scripts/data-governance-runtime-proof.ts. Proof level: 3 state-machine validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: No bespoke UI is shipped; V2 UI must derive from the API contract and DSR/catalog semantics in this semanticCompleteness record plus docs/api/openapi.json. The capability-ui-contract foundation asset makes this an explicit source requirement.

adapter: encrypted tar.gz archive builder + Postgres portable import applier
v2Target: reuse-and-extend: encrypted tar.gz export/import archive, manifest/checksum verification, SecretStore-wrapped data key, transactional group import, rollback, and resume semantics are proven

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Export lifecycle builds encrypted archive -> verifies manifest/digests -> imports ordered groups; import lifecycle records completedOrders, failedOrder, and resume state. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/data-portability.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Portable import progress state is completed_orders plus optional failed_order keyed by organisation/archive_digest; migration 044 persists durable progress. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/db/migrations/044-portable-import-progress.sql.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes are operator tenant export/import surfaces under /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/export and /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/import; permission recorded as tenant.data.export and route existence proven by proof:data-portability.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is encrypted tar.gz export/import route pair with root manifest, ordered entries, sha256 digests, wrapped data key reference, and tenant-bound verification. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/data-portability.ts; apps/platform-api/scripts/data-portability-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Tampered ciphertext rejects before import; entry order and digests are verified; cross-tenant domain conflicts roll back the group and record failed progress. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/data-portability.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Tamper rejection, group apply failure rollback, failedOrder progress, and cross-tenant domain conflict rollback are explicit. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/data-portability.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Capability audit action is export audited; portable import progress writes durable operational evidence without payload or secret material. Evidence: capability record, migration 044, and PostgresPortableTenantImportApplier tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Runtime readiness is proof:data-portability, validating route registration, encryption, SecretStore wrapping, tamper rejection, rollback, resume, and durable Postgres group application. Evidence: package.json script proof:data-portability.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/data-portability.test.ts. Runtime proof: apps/platform-api/scripts/data-portability-runtime-proof.ts. Proof level: 4 live-substrate-adjacent validation with durable Postgres adapter proof.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: No bespoke UI is shipped; V2 UI must expose export/import actions, archive verification state, failed group progress, and resume semantics from this record plus docs/api/openapi.json. The capability-ui-contract foundation asset makes this explicit.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Rate-limit lifecycle is explicit: operator sets a per-tenant policy, evaluation checks entitlement before counting, requests move within a fixed window from allowed below/at limit to denied above limit, and live counts reset in a new bucket. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/rate-limits.test.ts and rate-limits runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Policy state consists of policyKey, entitlementKey, limit, windowSeconds, action, updatedAt, updatedBy; counter state is tenant/policy/window used count with exceeded state when used > limit. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/ports/rate-limit-repository.ts and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/rate-limits.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Tenant developer read uses tenant.developer.read; operator rate-limit read/write use platform.rate_limits.read/write; policy evaluation also requires the configured entitlement. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/api-key-routes-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contracts are GET /api/org/rate-limits, GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/rate-limits, and PATCH /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/rate-limits, plus developer portal exposure through GET /api/org/developer. Evidence: api-key-routes runtime proof and docs/api/openapi.json.
semanticCompleteness.validation: No policy is opt-in allow; missing entitlement denies before counting; configured limits allow below/at limit and deny above; invalid tenant id returns 400 on operator route. Evidence: rate-limits unit test and api-key-routes runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: assertRateLimit throws once exceeded; evaluateRateLimit returns denied decisions by entitlement or exceeded state; Redis unavailable reports degraded and falls back to Postgres counting. Evidence: rate-limits tests and rate-limits-redis runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: setRateLimit emits rate_limit audit before mutation; audit event resource is rate_limit. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/rate-limits.test.ts and apps/platform-api/scripts/rate-limits-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Postgres proof skips honestly if unavailable; Redis provider readiness is ready on PING, degraded on Redis failure, with Postgres fallback still valid. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/rate-limits-runtime-proof.ts and apps/platform-api/scripts/rate-limits-redis-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/rate-limits.test.ts. Runtime proofs: proof:rate-limits, proof:rate-limits-redis, proof:api-key-routes. Proof levels: 4 for live Postgres and Redis substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose tenant rate-limit read state, operator policy table/form for policyKey entitlementKey limit windowSeconds action, live used count, exceeded/allowed state, degraded-provider messaging, permission gates, and invalid tenant/error presentation.

capability: Tenant lifecycle: provision, suspend, delete, export
contract: POST /api/admin/tenants plus tenant lifecycle suspend/delete/export usecases
v2Target: reuse-as-is: tenant lifecycle coordinates portable export, data, storage, realm, and DSR transitions

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Lifecycle operations are explicit: suspend coordinates data -> storage -> realm -> DSR after audit; delete exports first, then coordinates storage -> realm -> DSR -> data. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Tenant lifecycle state is represented by coordinated subsystem outcomes and export metadata: suspend returns coordinated [data, storage, realm, dsr]; delete returns coordinated [export, storage, realm, dsr, data] plus export digest/keyRef. Evidence: tenant lifecycle unit tests and runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Capability is operator-controlled by platform.tenants.* with system-admin actor context in proof and tests. Evidence: capability record and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts actor fixture.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract source is TenantLifecycleCoordinator with exportTenant, suspendData, suspendStorage, suspendRealm, suspendDsr, deleteData, deleteStorage, deleteRealm, deleteDsr plus admin tenant route family. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/tenant-lifecycle.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Delete refuses to continue after a subsystem coordination failure; failed deleteRealm stops before DSR/data deletion. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Subsystem failures propagate as typed/ordinary errors and stop later destructive steps; deleteRealm failure leaves calls at export, delete:storage, delete:realm. Evidence: tenant lifecycle unit test failure case.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Suspend and delete emit audit before subsystem coordination; unit and runtime proof assert audit invocation. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts; apps/platform-api/scripts/tenant-lifecycle-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Runtime readiness is npm run proof:tenant-lifecycle, which proves audit and subsystem coordination order for suspend and delete. Evidence: package.json script proof:tenant-lifecycle.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts. Runtime proof: apps/platform-api/scripts/tenant-lifecycle-runtime-proof.ts. Proof level: 3 state-machine/coordination validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: No new UI is required; V2 UI must expose suspend/delete/export lifecycle actions, audit-before-change expectations, export-first delete warning, and failure-stop semantics from this record and the TenantLifecycleCoordinator contract.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Host identity lifecycle is classify -> resolve: apex/reserved/invalid/malformed hosts stay global/no-tenant, tenant slug hosts resolve by organisation slug, active custom domains resolve by tenant_domains registry, and inactive/pending/disabled custom domains remain unresolved. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/domain-identity-matrix-runtime-proof.ts and tenant-custom-domain-resolution-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Host identity state uses kind apex|tenant_slug|reserved_subdomain|invalid_subdomain|custom_domain_candidate|malformed plus resolved tenant context containing organisationId, slug, realmName, and hostSource. Evidence: @platform/domain-identity and apps/platform-api/src/server/tenant-resolver.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Host identity is read-only request context; protected route permissions are enforced later by the pipeline, while global routes reject apex subdomains. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/pipeline.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract is GET /api/host-identity and implicit Host/X-Forwarded-Host request context consumed by the BFF pipeline. Evidence: route table and domain-identity-matrix proof.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Classification covers port stripping, reserved slugs, malformed hosts, unknown apex subdomains, custom-domain candidates, and X-Forwarded-Host precedence. Evidence: packages/domain-identity tests and domain-identity-matrix runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Untrusted or unresolved hosts return null tenant context; global routes reject apex subdomains with apex_subdomain detail instead of silently serving global content. Evidence: tenant-resolver and pipeline tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: No mutation audit applies because host identity resolution is a read-only derivation; failed resolution is logged by tenant-resolver/pipeline warnings. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/tenant-resolver.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready when resolver code and registry are available; runtime proof requires local Postgres for live resolution and skips only when no seeded organisation exists. Evidence: domain-identity-matrix runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs: proof:domain-identity-matrix and proof:tenant-custom-domain-resolution. Proof level: 4 live local Postgres resolver validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must consume host identity as context only: show resolved tenant/global state, prevent tenant UI on unresolved hosts, and present custom-domain source distinctly from slug source without granting permissions.

v2Target: reuse-as-is

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Activation lifecycle is inactive -> active only after verified ownership, audit emit, and AuthClientDomainPort.addRedirectOrigin success; deactivation active -> inactive removes the origin and clears canonical/routing state. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/tenant-domain-lifecycle.ts and tenant-domain-lifecycle unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State is tenant_domains.authClientStatus inactive|active plus authClientActivatedAt, canonical, routingStatus, and disabledAt guards. Evidence: TenantDomainRecord and tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Activation/deactivation require tenant.domains.write; the domain-only operation no longer requires tenant.auth.settings.write. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase comments and capability record.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is /api/org/domains/:id/activate and corresponding deactivation route, backed by activateDomainAuthClient/deactivateDomainAuthClient. Evidence: routes and usecase contract.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Activation refuses not_found, not_verified, and already_active before audit or external mutation; deactivation refuses not_found and not_active. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed result variants are ok, not_found, not_verified, already_active, and not_active; thrown AuthClientDomainPort errors leave registry inactive because markAuthClientActive is not called. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Activation and deactivation emit tenant_domains.auth_client.activated/deactivated before the Keycloak port mutation, with safe domain-only metadata. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Invariant-ready after verified ownership and successful auth-client mutation; activation does not imply routing or TLS readiness. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-domain-lifecycle.test.ts. Runtime proof: proof:tenant-domain-canonical. Proof level: 4 live registry validation with injected auth-client port.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose activate/deactivate actions only when permitted, show inactive/active states, block activation until ownership is verified, clear canonical/routing presentation after deactivation, and show typed guard failures.

v2Target: reuse-as-is
acceptablePartialRouteReason: Canonical cutover is proven in the self-contained local stack via redirect policy transition and redirectActive exposure; public DNS remains separately limited by V1C-09.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Canonical lifecycle is guarded set/unset: not_verified -> refused, auth_client_inactive -> refused, routing_not_proven -> refused, set flips canonical true and redirect_slug_to_canonical, second set atomically replaces the first, unset clears the flag, and deactivation clears canonical. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State is canonical boolean plus canonicalAt and redirectPolicy, constrained to at most one enabled canonical row per tenant; canonical does not upgrade routingStatus or tlsStatus. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof and tenant_domains registry.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Canonical set/unset require tenant.domains.write. Evidence: capability record and tenant-domain lifecycle usecases.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is /api/org/domains/canonical, backed by setCanonicalDomain and unsetCanonicalDomain. Evidence: capability record and apps/platform-api/src/usecases/tenant-domain-lifecycle.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Set canonical requires verified ownership, active auth client, and proven local routing; unset requires existing domain; second canonical replacement preserves the one-canonical invariant. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed result variants are ok, not_found, not_verified, auth_client_inactive, and routing_not_proven. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Set/unset emit tenant_domains.canonical.set/unset audit events with domain and redirectPolicy metadata before registry mutation. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase and unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is local canonical cutover only; public DNS/TLS remain explicitly deferred and canonical is a marker that does not imply public routing/TLS readiness. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proof: proof:tenant-domain-canonical validates live registry guards, redirect policy, atomic replacement, unset, and deactivation clearing. Proof level: 4 live substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose one canonical marker per tenant, set/unset actions, guard-specific disabled/error states, redirect policy state, public DNS/TLS deferral labels, and canonical clearing after deactivation.

v2Target: carry-forward-blocked: derivation reused; live brokered login external
externalProofLimitation: Live brokered login on a custom domain requires a real external IdP and is not provable in the self-contained V2 stack. The derivation is delivered and proven (proof:tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin); the live end-to-end proof is carried as the separate not-applicable-final capability [Real IdP login simulation] / [OIDC login simulation]. Not a V2-cut blocker.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Auth callback derivation lifecycle is request host -> tenant resolution -> login redirect derivation: active custom domains use tenant realm and custom callback origin; verified-but-inactive and unknown custom hosts fall back to platform realm; slug hosts keep tenant realm on slug origin. Evidence: tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State depends on tenant_domains ownershipStatus verified and authClientStatus active plus resolved realmName tenant-{orgId}; inactive/unknown custom host state is untrusted for callback derivation. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Domain callback origin eligibility is controlled by tenant.domains.write lifecycle operations; login derivation itself is request-context based and does not grant tenant permissions without authentication. Evidence: tenant-domain lifecycle and auth handler proof.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract is derived realm/redirect_uri for /auth/login and /auth/callback, with redirect_uri=https://{custom}/auth/callback only for active custom domains. Evidence: handleAuthLogin runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Active custom domain must be verified and auth-client active; verified-but-inactive and unknown custom domains cannot claim callback origin; slug hosts remain valid. Evidence: tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Inactive or unknown custom hosts degrade to platform realm/callback instead of throwing or trusting the custom host; real IdP token exchange remains separately not-applicable in the self-contained stack. Evidence: tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin proof and externalProofLimitation.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: The derivation path is read-only; audit applies to the domain activation/deactivation mutations that make the callback origin eligible. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase audit events.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is deterministic derivation proof for local Postgres/Redis; live brokered login is external and explicitly carried as not-applicable-final rather than fake-passed. Evidence: tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proof: proof:tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin validates real handleAuthLogin against live local Postgres and Redis for active, inactive, unknown, and slug host cases. Proof level: 4 live handler/substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must show custom-domain auth callback eligibility only for active domains, display the exact callback origin, explain inactive/unknown fallback states, and keep real IdP login proof separate from derivation readiness.

contract: POST /faro/collect (Alloy faro.receiver via Caddy); OTLP browser_traces -> Tempo
adapter: Grafana Faro web SDK + OTEL TracingInstrumentation; Alloy faro.receiver -> otel-collector -> Tempo/Loki
